info.title: Disbursements
info.description: Automatically deposit funds to multiple users

get.description: Get the balance of own account.

parameters.name: Authorization
parameters.description: Bearer Authentication Token generated using CreateAccessToken API Call

parameters.name: X-Target-Environment
parameters.description: The identifier of the Wallet Platform system where the transaction shall be processed. This parameter is used to route the request to the Wallet Platform system that will initiate the transaction.

400.description: Bad request, e.g. invalid data was sent in the request.

500.description: Internal error. The returned response contains details.

Incorrect target environment.message: Access to target environment is forbidden.

get.description: Operation is used  to check if an account holder is registered and active in the system.

parameters.name: accountHolderId
parameters.description:
The AccountHolder number. Validated according to the AccountHolderID type.
MSISDN - Mobile Number validated according to ITU-T E.164. Validated with IsMSISDN
EMAIL - Validated to be a valid e-mail format. Validated with IsEmail

parameters.name: accountHolderIdType
parameters.description: Specifies the type of the AccountHolderID. Allowed values [msisdn, email]

parameters.name: Authorization
parameters.description: Bearer Authentication Token generated using CreateAccessToken API Call

parameters.name: X-Target-Environment
parameters.description: The identifier of the Wallet Platform system where the transaction shall be processed. This parameter is used to route the request to the Wallet Platform system that will initiate the transaction.

200.description: Ok. True if account holder is registered and active, false if the account holder is not active or not found found

400.description: Bad request, e.g. invalid data was sent in the request.

500.description: Internal error. The returned response contains details.

Incorrect target environment.message: Access to target environment is forbidden.

get.description: This operation is used to get the status of a transfer. X-Reference-Id that was passed in the post is used as reference to the request.

parameters.name: referenceId
parameters.description: UUID of transaction to get result. Reference id  used when creating the Transfer.

parameters.name: Authorization
parameters.description: Bearer Authentication Token generated using CreateAccessToken API Call

parameters.name: X-Target-Environment
parameters.description: The identifier of the Wallet Platform system where the transaction shall be processed. This parameter is used to route the request to the Wallet Platform system that will initiate the transaction.

200.description: OK. Note that a failed transfer will be returned with this status too. The 'status' of the TransferResult can be used to determine the outcome of the request. The 'reason' field can be used to retrieve a cause in case of failure.

reason.message: The payer's limit has been breached.
The payer does not have enough funds.

400.description: Bad request, e.g. an incorrectly formatted reference id was provided.

500.description: Internal Error. Note that if the retreieved transfer has failed, it will not cause this status to be returned. This status is only returned if the GET request itself fails.

Unspecified internal error.message: An internal error occurred while processing.

get.description: This operation returns personal information of the account holder. The operation does not need any consent by the account holder.

parameters.name: accountHolderIdType
parameters.description: Type of account holder identity passed in accountHolderId path param.Possible values:MSISDN Email AliasID (account ID)

parameters.name: accountHolderId
parameters.description: string

parameters.name: Authorization
parameters.description: Bearer Authentication Token generated using CreateAccessToken API Call

parameters.name: X-Target-Environment
parameters.description: The identifier of the Wallet Platform system where the transaction shall be processed. This parameter is used to route the request to the Wallet Platform system that will initiate the transaction.

post.description: This operation is used to claim a consent by the account holder for the requested scopes.

parameters.name: Authorization
parameters.description: Basic authentication header containing API user ID and API key. Should be sent in as B64 encoded.

parameters.name: X-Target-Environment
parameters.description: The identifier of the Wallet Platform system where the transaction shall be processed. This parameter is used to route the request to the Wallet Platform system that will initiate the transaction.

parameters.name: X-Callback-Url
parameters.description: URL to the server where the callback should be sent.

get.description: Get the balance of own account. Currency parameter passed in GET

parameters.name: currency
parameters.description: Should be in ISO4217 Currency

parameters.name: Authorization
parameters.description: Bearer Authentication Token generated using CreateAccessToken API Call

parameters.name: X-Target-Environment
parameters.description: The identifier of the Wallet Platform system where the transaction shall be processed. This parameter is used to route the request to the Wallet Platform system that will initiate the transaction.

400.description: Bad request, e.g. invalid data was sent in the request.

500.description: Internal error. The returned response contains details.

Incorrect target environment.message: Access to target environment is forbidden.

post.description:
deposit operation is used to deposit an amount from the owner’s account to a payee account.
Status of the transaction can be validated by using the GET /deposit/\{referenceId\}

parameters.name: Authorization
parameters.description: Bearer Authentication Token generated using CreateAccessToken API Call

parameters.name: X-Callback-Url
parameters.description: (POST Method)URL to the server where the callback should be sent.

parameters.name: X-Reference-Id
parameters.description: Format - UUID. Recource ID of the created ‘request-to-pay’ transaction. This ID is used for e.g. validating the status of the request. Universal Unique ID for the transaction generated using UUID version 4.

parameters.name: X-Target-Environment
parameters.description: The identifier of the Wallet Platform system where the transaction shall be processed. This parameter is used to route the request to the Wallet Platform system that will initiate the transaction.

400.description: Bad request, e.g. invalid data was sent in the request.

ReferenceId already in use.message: Duplicated reference id. Creation of resource failed.

get.description: This operation is used to claim a consent by the account holder for the requested scopes.

parameters.name: Authorization
parameters.description: Bearer Token. Replace with a valid oauth2 token received from oauth2 token endpoint in Wallet Platform.

parameters.name: X-Target-Environment
parameters.description: The identifier of the Wallet Platform system where the transaction shall be processed. This parameter is used to route the request to the Wallet Platform system that will initiate the transaction.

post.description: This operation is used to create an access token which can then be used to authorize and authenticate towards the other end-points of the API.

parameters.name: Authorization
parameters.description: Basic authentication header containing API user ID and API key. Should be sent in as B64 encoded.

post.description:
Transfer operation is used to transfer an amount from the own account to a payee account.
Status of the transaction can validated by using the GET /transfer/\{referenceId\}

parameters.name: Authorization
parameters.description: Bearer Authentication Token generated using CreateAccessToken API Call

parameters.name: X-Callback-Url
parameters.description: URL to the server where the callback should be sent.

parameters.name: X-Reference-Id
parameters.description: Format - UUID. Recource ID of the created request to pay transaction. This ID is used, for example validating the status of the request. ‘Universal Unique ID’ for the transaction generated using UUID version 4.

parameters.name: X-Target-Environment
parameters.description: The identifier of the Wallet Platform system where the transaction shall be processed. This parameter is used to route the request to the Wallet Platform system that will initiate the transaction.

400.description: Bad request, e.g. invalid data was sent in the request.

ReferenceId already in use.message: Duplicated reference id. Creation of resource failed.

login_hint.description: The identity of the account holder.

consent_valid_in.description: The validity time of the consent in secondsThis parameter can only be used together with access type offline.

client_notification_token.description: This token is required when the client is using Ping or Push mode.

scope_instruction.description: Base64 encoded Instrcution of the financial transaction.

auth_req_id.description: Authentication request ID as an UUID.

interval.description: Indicates how long time the client should wait between retries towards the endpoint /oauth2/token.

expires_in.description: Shows when the authentication request ID expires, in seconds.

given_name.description: Given name(s) or first name(s) of the End-User. Note that in some cultures, people can have multiple given names; all can be present, with the names being separated by space characters.

family_name.description: Surname(s) or last name(s) of the End-User. Note that in some cultures, people can have multiple family names or no family name; all can be present, with the names being separated by space characters.

locale.description: End-User's locale, represented as a  BCP47 [RFC5646] language tag. This is typically an  ISO 639-1 Alpha-2 [ISO639�|�1] language code in lowercase and an  ISO 3166-1 Alpha-2 [ISO3166�|�1] country code in uppercase, separated by a dash. For example,  en-US or  fr-CA. As a compatibility note, some implementations have used an underscore as the separator rather than a dash, for example,  en_US; Relying Parties may choose to accept this locale syntax as well.

gender.description: End-User's gender. Values defined by this specification are female and male. Other values may be used when neither of the defined values are applicable.

access_token.description: A JWT token which can be used to authrize against the other API end-points. The format of the token follows the JWT standard format (see jwt.io for an example). This is the token that should be sent in in the Authorization header when calling the other API end-points.

expires_in.description: The validity time in seconds of the token.

grant_type.description: Value ca be either "urn:openid:params:grant-type:ciba" or refresh_token

auth_req_id.description: Authentication request ID.Value is only mandatory if grant_type is "urn:openid:params:grant-type:ciba"

refresh_token.description: UUID.Refresh token retrieved from oauth2 token endpoint for consents with grant_type offline. This parameter is only valid if grant_type is refresh_token.

access_token.description: Oauth2 JWT access token.The generated token is valid 3600 seconds as default.

expires_in.description: Shows when the authentication request ID expires, in seconds.

scope.description: List of scopes that belongs to the authentication request ID.

refresh_token_expired_in.description: The time in seconds until the consent can no longer be refreshed. Based on the default value for consent validity, or the value set to parameter consent_valid_in sent in the bc-authorize request.

sub.description: Subject - Identifier for the End-User at the Issuer.

name.description: End-User's full name in displayable form including all name parts.

given_name.description: Given name(s) or first name(s) of the End-User.

family_name.description: Surname(s) or last name(s) of the End-User.

email.description: End-User's preferred e-mail address. Its value MUST conform to the  RFC 5322 [RFC5322] address specification syntax.

email_verified.description: The response value is True if the End-User's e-mail address has been verified;otherwise false.

phone_number_verified.description: The response value is True if the End-User's phone number has been verified; otherwise false.

updated_at.description: The time the End-User's information was last updated.

birthdate.description: The birth date of the account holder.

credit_score.description: The credit score of the account holder.

active.description: The status of the account holder.

region_of_birth.description: The birth region of the account holder.

city_of_birth.description: The city of birth for the account holder.

identification_type.description: Type of identification.The first non-expired identification is always chosen.

formatted.description: Full mailing address, formatted for display or use on a mailing label. This field may contain multiple lines, separated by newlines.

street_address.description: Full street address component, which may include house number, street name, Post Office Box, and multi-line extended street address information.

region.description: State, province, prefecture, or region component.

postal_code.description: Zip code or postal code component.

Balance.description: The available balance of the account

availableBalance.description: The available balance of the account

Party.description:
Party identifies a account holder in the wallet platform. Party consists of two parameters, type and partyId. Each type have its own validation of the partyId
MSISDN - Mobile Number validated according to ITU-T E.164. Validated with IsMSISDN
EMAIL - Validated to be a valid e-mail format. Validated with IsEmail
PARTY_CODE - UUID of the party. Validated with IsUuid

payerMessage.description: The mesage that is shown to the approver.

validityTime.description: The request validity time of the pre-approval

payerMessage.description: The mesage that is shown to the approver.

validityTime.description: The request validity time of the pre-approval

amount.description: Amount that will be debited from the payer account.

externalId.description:
External id is used as a reference to the transaction. External id is used for reconciliation. The external id will be included in transaction history report.
External id is not required to be unique.
